rationale: Re-review after PR #40 fixes (fd78dd2): DI-15 prose gained the user_needs-registry/runbook parity claim and the test was strengthened to match. All probes executed via rdm story mutation-probe against test_init_scaffolds_a_project. Clause table: (1) one command lays down templates/Makefile/config -- gut init's copytree (mkdir only): KILLED by the Makefile/config.yml/documents assertions. (2) design-controls templates ship -- copytree then unlink the scaffolded software_design_specification.md: KILLED by its is_file assertion (design_review.md and traceability_matrix.md asserted alongside). (3) the scaffolded V&V plan carries a machine-readable user_needs registry -- rename 'user_needs: []' to 'user_need_registry: []' in the shipped template rdm/init_files/documents/verification_and_validation_plan.md: KILLED by the yaml.safe_load + frontmatter['user_needs'] == [] assertion (present, empty, parseable). (4) AGENT_WORKFLOW.md lands at the project root, copied from the adopt tree at init time -- copy the runbook to 'WORKFLOW.md' instead: KILLED by the (project / 'AGENT_WORKFLOW.md').is_file() assertion. Documented hole (SURVIVED, not an equivalent mutant): mutating the shipped template's frontmatter line 'kind: design' to 'kind: prose' in software_design_specification.md survives, because the test's substring check '"kind: design" in read_text()' is also satisfied by a template comment ('Discovery keys on `kind: design` ...') on line 8 of the same file. The template's presence is verified (clause 2 probe), but the frontmatter-key integrity of its content is only substring-checked; a future strengthening should parse the frontmatter. This is a sub-property beyond DI-15's laying-down clauses, all of which have killing probes, so the verdict stays faithful with the hole recorded honestly.
